rulings.question: Can the keep-create-benchmark ruling be refuted as a near-empty shell?
rulings.ruling: No. Keep create-benchmark as a distinct workflow packet, but define it narrowly as the MCP benchmark-folder promotion lifecycle, not as a /create:* command.
rulings.rationale: The workflow has a distinct entry condition, output contract, and authoring sequence: it only applies when a skill has an MCP server with a measurable surface and a completed, reproducible benchmark packet; it produces mcp_server/benchmarks/README.md plus dated benchmark folders containing benchmark_report.md, results.csv, optional per-probe/runtime artifacts, and SOURCE.md; and it requires copy/promote/write/validate/index steps rather than generic doc authoring.

rulings.question: Does assets/benchmark prove a distinct output contract rather than just shared authoring guidance?
rulings.ruling: Yes. The templates encode two required artifacts with different jobs: benchmark_report.md is the ten-section curated benchmark narrative, and SOURCE.md is the wayfinding pointer back to the authoritative spec packet.
rulings.rationale: benchmark_report_template.md contains the concrete copy target under mcp_server/benchmarks/benchmark-<YYYY-MM-DD>/benchmark_report.md, preserves fixed numbered sections including aggregate results, methodology, findings, caveats, reproducibility, and related resources; source_template.md separately defines the spec-packet pointer, evidence map, follow-on packets, and update triggers.

rulings.question: Is there real usage beyond an aspirational template?
rulings.ruling: Yes, enough to defeat the default-fold rule.
rulings.rationale: The reference documents shipped benchmark examples in system-spec-kit and system-code-graph, explains why each adoption occurred, and records what the promoted folder carried. Grep also shows sk-doc currently routes BENCHMARK intent to benchmark_creation.md plus both templates, so this is existing monolith behavior to preserve rather than a new empty extension.

rulings.question: Does the lack of a bound /create:benchmark command force folding into shared guides?
rulings.ruling: No, but it is a naming and routing risk.
rulings.rationale: The parent packet itself flags create-benchmark as provisional because it has no bound command and few repo uses; however, command binding is not the only lifecycle boundary here. The durable boundary is the MCP benchmark promotion contract plus existing BENCHMARK intent resources.

risks: Do not conflate create-benchmark with the separate Lane C skill-benchmark harness; the former authors mcp_server/benchmarks/benchmark-<YYYY-MM-DD>/ artifacts, while the latter emits skill-benchmark-report.{json,md} for parent-hub routing benchmarks.
The packet needs explicit router wording because /create:benchmark does not exist; users may otherwise expect a slash-command workflow.
If implementation folds assets/benchmark into shared, preserve facade paths or update all resource-map/link checks that currently load benchmark_creation.md plus both templates.
open: Confirm whether the parent router should expose the mode as create-benchmark or benchmark-creation to avoid implying a /create:benchmark command.
